The function will replace all instances of a URL pattern with a new one. It will handle repeating strings and maintain the structure of the original text. The output will be a clean, compliant string with no punctuation.  

The function will be called `replace_url` and will take two parameters: the original string and the replacement URL. It will use a regular expression to find and replace all occurrences of the URL pattern.  

The function will ensure that the replacement is accurate and that the structure of the original string is preserved. It will not alter the meaning or appearance of the text.  

The function will return the modified string as the final output. It will be tested with multiple examples to ensure correctness and compliance.  

The function will be simple, efficient, and easy to use. It will handle all cases, including repeated URLs and nested structures.  

The function will be wrapped in a title as specified, and the response will start and end with the same word.  

The response will be under 36 sentences and will avoid the use of dots. It will contain a palindrome in the final output.
